summary: Register for an ABN before invoicing or trading — it identifies your business to the ATO and other businesses, and is required to register for GST, open a business bank account, and avoid pay-as-you-go withholding on payments you receive.
You must register for GST within 21 days of your GST turnover exceeding $75,000 in a 12-month period. Once registered you charge GST on taxable sales and can claim GST credits on business purchases.

The ACL applies consumer guarantees that cannot be excluded by any contract term — your refund, warranty, and advertising practices must not misrepresent these rights. Misleading or deceptive conduct is enforced by the ACCC.

If you collect personal information from customers and either have turnover above $3 million or handle certain sensitive categories, the Privacy Act and Australian Privacy Principles apply. A clear, compliant privacy policy is good practice regardless of turnover.

item: Business name registration (if trading under a name other than your own)
summary: If you trade under any name other than your own personal legal name, you must register that business name with ASIC before you start using it.

Not a statutory requirement for most sole traders, but strongly advised — it covers claims for property damage or injury caused to a third party in the course of doing business, and many commercial clients or venues will not engage you without it.
